title: Exocarpos epiphyllanthus
taxonomy: Exocarpos epiphyllanthus is classified within the plant family Santalaceae. This family is a group of parasitic flowering plants commonly known as mistletoes and sandalwoods. The genus Exocarpos is a group of epiphytic shrubs native to Australia and other parts of the world. Exocarpos epiphyllanthus is one of the species within this genus. It is a type of parasitic plant that grows on the branches of other trees.

intro: Exocarpus epiphyllanthus, also known as the Australian sandalwood, is a small, woody shrub native to Western Australia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Exocarpos epiphyllanthus include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Exocarpos epiphyllanthus is often used to make teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are valued for their potential to soothe respiratory issues and reduce inflammation.
